curriculum.subject: Biology
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 26 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

units.description: The Living World, Biological Classification, Plant Kingdom, Animal Kingdom

units.unitName: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
units.description: Morphology of Flowering Plants, Anatomy of Flowering Plants, Structural Organisation in Animals
Cell-The Unit of Life, Biomolecules, Cell Cycle and Cell Division
Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Respiration in Plants, Plant - Growth and Development
Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Human Reproduction, Reproductive Health
Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Evolution
Human Health and Diseases, Microbes in Human Welfare
Biotechnology - Principles and Processes, Biotechnology and its Applications
Organisms and Populations, Ecosystem, Biodiversity and its Conservation

key_concepts: Biodiversity and Need for classification

Concept of species and taxonomical hierarchy
Binomial nomenclature
Five kingdom classification system

Classification into major groups: Lichens, Viruses and Viroids
Classification of plants into major groups

Classification of chordates upto class level

units.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

key_concepts: Morphology of root, stem, leaf, inflorescence, flower, fruit and seed
Anatomy and functions of tissue systems in dicots
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of digestive system (frog)
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of circulatory system (frog)
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of respiratory system (frog)
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of nervous system (frog)
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of reproductive system (frog)
Cell theory and cell as the basic unit of life
Structure of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; Plant vs Animal cell
Structure and function of proteins, carbohydrates, lipids, nucleic acids
The cell cycle
Site of photosynthesis and pigments involved
Cellular respiration: glycolysis, fermentation (anaerobic), TCA cycle
Seed germination; phases of plant growth
Flower structure and gametophyte development
Pollination types, agencies, and out-breeding devices
Pollen-pistil interaction and double fertilization
Post-fertilization events: endosperm, embryo, seed, fruit formation
Special modes: apomixis, parthenocarpy, polyembryony
Male and female reproductive systems and microscopic anatomy
Gametogenesis: spermatogenesis and oogenesis
Menstrual cycle, fertilization, and blastocyst formation
Implantation, pregnancy, placenta formation (elementary)
Parturition and lactation (elementary)
Need for reproductive health and STD prevention
Birth control methods, contraception, and MTP
Amniocentesis procedures
Infertility and assisted reproductive technologies (IVF, ZIFT, GIFT)
Mendelian inheritance and deviations (incomplete dominance, co-dominance)
Multiple alleles, blood groups, pleiotropy, polygenic inheritance
Chromosome theory of inheritance and sex determination mechanisms
Linkage, crossing over, and sex-linked inheritance disorders
Mendelian and chromosomal disorders in humans
Search for genetic material and DNA structure (DNA/RNA)
DNA packaging, replication, and Central Dogma processes
Transcription, genetic code, translation, gene expression regulation
Lac operon model of gene regulation
Genome projects and DNA fingerprinting
Origin of life and evidences for evolution (paleontology, anatomy, embryology)
Darwin's contribution and modern synthetic theory
Mechanisms of evolution: variation via mutation and recombination

formative_content_refs.title: Spotting and Observation B (Microscope, Specimens, Models)

practicals.title: Study and describe locally available common flowering plants
practicals.details: Family Solanaceae (Poaceae, Asteraceae or Brassicaceae can be substituted). Dissection and display of floral whorls, anther and ovary to show number of chambers. Floral formulae and diagrams. Type of root (tap and adventitious), stem (herbaceous and woody), leaf (arrangement, shape, venation, simple and compound).

practicals.title: Preparation and study of T.S. of dicot and monocot roots and stems
practicals.details: Primary structure.

practicals.title: Study of osmosis by potato osmometer
practicals.details: Experiment No. 3

practicals.title: Study of plasmolysis in epidermal peels
practicals.details: Using Rhoeo/lily leaves or flashy scale leaves of onion bulb.

practicals.title: Study of distribution of stomata on the upper and lower surfaces of leaves
Comparative study of the rates of transpiration in the upper and lower surfaces of leaves
Test for the presence of sugar, starch, proteins and fats
practicals.details: In suitable plant and animal materials.

practicals.title: Separation of plant pigments through paper chromatography
Study of the rate of respiration in flower buds/leaf tissue and germinating seeds
Test for presence of urea in urine
Test for presence of sugar in urine
Test for presence of albumin in urine
Test for presence of bile salts in urine
Study locally available common flowering plants of the family Solanaceae (Visually Impaired)
practicals.details: Identify type of stem (Herbaceous or Woody), type of leaves (Compound or Simple).

practicals.title: Study the parts of a compound microscope
practicals.details: Eye piece and objective lens, mirror, stage, coarse and fine adjustment knobs.

practicals.title: Differentiate between monocot and dicot plants on the basis of venation patterns
Study parts of human skeleton (Model)
practicals.details: Ball and socket joints of thigh and shoulder; Rib cage.

practicals.title: Study honeybee/butterfly, snail/sheik snail through shell, Starfish, Pigeon
practicals.details: Through models (Visually Impaired).

practicals.title: Identify the given specimen of a fungus and gymnosperm
practicals.details: Mushroom, pine cone.

practicals.title: Relate experimental set up with aim (Potato Osmometer/endosmosis in raisins)
Prepare a temporary mount to observe pollen germination
Study the plant population density by quadrat method
Study the plant population frequency by quadrat method
Prepare a temporary mount of onion root tip to study mitosis
Isolate DNA from available plant material
practicals.details: Spinach, green pea seeds, papaya, banana etc.

projects.title: Investigatory Project
projects.details: Project Record + Viva Voce (Credit to the student's work over the academic session may be given)

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XI Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting Part B: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.

Class XII Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
question_paper_design: Class XI (2026-27) Theory Question Paper Design: Typology includes VSA including MCQs, Assertion–Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II; Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions. Internal choice of approximately 33% provided.

Class XII (2026-27) Theory Question Paper Design: Typology includes VSA including MCQs, Assertion–Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II; Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions. Internal choice of approximately 33% provided.
competency_weightage: Class XI Theory: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%), Application of Knowledge / Concepts (30%), Analyse, Evaluate and Create (20%).

Class XII Theory: Competencies mapped to question types. Suggestive verbs for 'Demonstrate Knowledge' include State, name, list, identify, define; for 'Application' include Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain; for 'Analyse, Evaluate and Create' include Interpret, analyse, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate.
bloom_levels: Level 1: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%). Verbs: State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize.
Level 2: Application of Knowledge / Concepts (30%). Verbs: Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish.
Level 3: Analyse, Evaluate and Create (20%). Verbs: Interpret, analyse, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct.
internal_assessment: Class XI Formative Assessment Topics: Digestion and Absorption. These topics are included in the syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

Class XII Formative Assessment Topics: Environmental Issues (Air pollution, Water pollution, Solid Wastes, Agro-chemicals, Radioactive wastes, Greenhouse effect, Ozone depletion, Degradation by improper resource utilization). These topics are included in the syllabus but assessed only formatively.
practical_assessment: Class XI Practical Examination for Visually Impaired Students: Time 2 Hours, Max Marks 30. Components: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks), Practical Records (5 marks), Viva (10 marks).

Class XII Practical Examination for Visually Impaired Students: Time 2 Hours, Max Marks 30. Components: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks), Practical Records (5 marks), Viva (10 marks).
project_assessment: Class XI Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.
Class XII Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.

competency_groups.title: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
competencies: C-3.1 Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes

C-3.2 Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction
C-3.3 Describes mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA)

competency_groups.title: Explores interconnectedness between organisms and their environment
competencies: C-4.1 Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic/ heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five-kingdoms
C-4.2 Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C-4.3 Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C-4.4 Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level (using models and/or simulations)

C-4.5 Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection in terms of changes — in allele frequency in population, structure, and function of organisms

competency_groups.title: Draws linkages between scientific knowledge and knowledge across other curricular areas
competencies: C-5.3 Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)

competency_groups.title: Understands and appreciates the contribution of India through history and the present times to the overall field of Science, including the disciplines that constitute it
competencies: C-6.1 Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) that are studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner

competency_groups.title: Develops awareness of the most current discoveries, ideas, and frontiers in all areas of scientific knowledge in order to appreciate that Science is ever evolving, and that there are still many unanswered questions
competencies: C-7.1 States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied — ranging from mere familiarity to conceptual understanding of the matter as appropriate to the developmental stage of the students
C-7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised to be inadequate

competency_groups.title: Explores the nature of Science by doing Science
competencies: C-8.1 Develops accurate and appropriate models (including geometric, mathematical, graphical) to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles and use these models to manipulate variables and predict results

C-8.2 Designs and implements a plan for scientific inquiry (formulates hypotheses, makes predictions, identifies variables, accurately uses scientific instruments, represents data — primary and secondary — in multiple modes, draws inferences based on data and understanding of scientific concepts, theories, laws, and principles, communicates findings using scientific terminology)

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XI Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting Part B: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.

Class XII Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
question_paper_design: Class XI (2026-27) Theory Question Paper Design: Typology includes VSA including MCQs, Assertion–Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II; Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions. Internal choice of approximately 33% provided.

Class XII (2026-27) Theory Question Paper Design: Typology includes VSA including MCQs, Assertion–Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II; Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions. Internal choice of approximately 33% provided.
competency_weightage: Class XI Theory: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%), Application of Knowledge / Concepts (30%), Analyse, Evaluate and Create (20%).

Class XII Theory: Competencies mapped to question types. Suggestive verbs for 'Demonstrate Knowledge' include State, name, list, identify, define; for 'Application' include Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain; for 'Analyse, Evaluate and Create' include Interpret, analyse, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate.
bloom_levels: Level 1: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%). Verbs: State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize.
Level 2: Application of Knowledge / Concepts (30%). Verbs: Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish.
Level 3: Analyse, Evaluate and Create (20%). Verbs: Interpret, analyse, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct.
internal_assessment: Class XI Formative Assessment Topics: Digestion and Absorption. These topics are included in the syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

Class XII Formative Assessment Topics: Environmental Issues (Air pollution, Water pollution, Solid Wastes, Agro-chemicals, Radioactive wastes, Greenhouse effect, Ozone depletion, Degradation by improper resource utilization). These topics are included in the syllabus but assessed only formatively.
practical_assessment: Class XI Practical Examination for Visually Impaired Students: Time 2 Hours, Max Marks 30. Components: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks), Practical Records (5 marks), Viva (10 marks).

Class XII Practical Examination for Visually Impaired Students: Time 2 Hours, Max Marks 30. Components: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks), Practical Records (5 marks), Viva (10 marks).
project_assessment: Class XI Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.
Class XII Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.

projects.title: Investigatory Project
projects.details: Project Record + Viva Voce (Credit to the student's work over the academic session may be given)

practicals.title: Study and describe locally available common flowering plants
practicals.details: Family Solanaceae (Poaceae, Asteraceae or Brassicaceae can be substituted). Dissection and display of floral whorls, anther and ovary to show number of chambers. Floral formulae and diagrams. Type of root (tap and adventitious), stem (herbaceous and woody), leaf (arrangement, shape, venation, simple and compound).

practicals.details: Primary structure.

practicals.title: Study of osmosis by potato osmometer
practicals.details: Experiment No. 3

practicals.title: Study of plasmolysis in epidermal peels
practicals.details: Using Rhoeo/lily leaves or flashy scale leaves of onion bulb.
